qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Uncontrolled recursion with O(n^2) algorithmic complexity in JSON Schema parser enables denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same package and denial-of-service theme, but the finding targets the JSON Schema converter path Grammar.from_json_schema()/cpp/json_schema_converter.cc, while the advisory targets raw nested grammar rules compiled by GrammarCompiler.compile_grammar().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-7rgv-gqhr-fxg3 describes xgrammar denial of service from a malicious raw grammar rule containing tens of thousands of nested parentheses that crashes during GrammarCompiler.compile_grammar(); the qualifying xgrammar finding instead covers deeply nested JSON Schema anyOf input through Grammar.from_json_schema() and cpp/json_schema_converter.cc.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7rgv-gqhr-fxg3 PoC builds grammar = "root ::= " + 30000 nested parentheses + "a" and calls compiler.compile_grammar(math_grammar), resulting in a segmentation fault.

uncontrolled-recursion-json-schema/vulnerability_report.json identifies Grammar.from_json_schema(), GrammarCompiler.compile_json_schema(), SchemaParser::Parse(), and ComputeCacheKey in cpp/json_schema_converter.cc as the vulnerable path.
